title: Cloud on-demand installation-token pull (generacy-cloud#817 — client view)
description: Client-side view of the cloud endpoint this consumer calls. Source-of-truth for the wire format lives in generacy-cloud#817; this schema is what packages/control-plane validates. Drift between this schema and the actual endpoint manifests as a CLOUD_RESPONSE_INVALID error (intentional — fail-loud).

Request.title: CloudPullRequest
Request.description: Cloud endpoint TBD on #817 (assumed POST /api/clusters/git-installation-token or similar). Authenticated via `Authorization: Bearer <cluster-api-key>` header read from /var/lib/generacy/cluster-api-key.

credentialId.description: The cluster-side credential ID. Cloud uses this together with the authenticated cluster identity to find the right GitHub App installation.

SuccessResponse.title: CloudPullResponse
SuccessResponse.description: Expected 200 application/json body. Anything else → CLOUD_RESPONSE_INVALID.

token.description: Fresh GitHub installation token. Caller treats this as opaque.

expiresAt.description: RFC-3339 / ISO-8601 UTC. GitHub installation tokens are capped at 1h from issuance.

client_error_mapping.description: How packages/control-plane/src/services/cloud-pull-client.ts maps HTTP outcomes to GitHelperErrorCode.

mappings.condition: network/DNS error or ECONNREFUSED before any response
2xx but body fails this schema or expiresAt is in the past

open_items: Exact path on the cloud is TBD on generacy-cloud#817; the client is structured so the URL is a single constant to update.
Authorization header format assumed `Bearer <key>`; align with cloud team during integration.
If cloud returns a username alongside the token, the client ignores it — username is always the constant `x-access-token` (FR-012, Q5).
